role_hints.oews_basis: role_mix is the 5-digit 54171 proxy, biased toward biotech: 19-1042 Medical Scientists 6.1% + 19-1021 Biochemists/Biophysicists 2.2% listed, but real nanotech firms skew to 19-2032 Materials Scientists, 19-2031 Chemists and 19-2012 Physicists in the unlisted tail, est. ~15-25%
role_hints.note: Literature review, experiment design drafts, simulation setup, data analysis and paper/report/grant writing are heavily AI-exposed; physical synthesis, characterization runs and instrument work are not. Plausible within-role fraction 0.2-0.4.

role_hints.oews_basis: 17-2141 Mechanical Engineers 1.9% listed + electrical/chemical/materials engineering occupations in the tail, est. ~10-15% more
role_hints.note: Design, modeling and documentation automate substantially; fab/cleanroom process work and equipment integration are physical.

role_hints.oews_basis: 19-4021 Biological Technicians 1.5% listed (proxy artifact) + chemical/engineering technicians in the tail, est. ~6-10%
role_hints.note: Hands-on sample prep, instrument operation and cleanroom work; lab-automation-augmented but physically present.

role_hints.oews_basis: 11-9121 Natural Sciences Managers 7.3% + 11-9199 Managers All Other 3.1%
role_hints.note: Sponsor/customer relationships and program direction persist; internal reporting and proposal assembly automate.

role_hints.oews_basis: 11-1021 General and Operations Managers 5.2% + 13-1082 Project Management Specialists 2.6% + 13-1199 Business Operations Specialists 1.6%
role_hints.note: Grant/contract administration, compliance paperwork and project tracking automate heavily.
Simulation, instrument control and data pipelines; AI-augmented rather than eliminated.

role_hints.oews_basis: not in listed top-10; 54171 top-10 covers only ~38% of wage bill — admin, facilities, QA and misc. occupations
role_hints.note: Admin/document/compliance work automates; facilities and EHS staff for labs/cleanrooms do not.

source_hints.source: NSF Business Enterprise Research and Development (BERD) Survey
source_hints.why: Official data on business R&D activity and composition — frames what this code's firms actually do and how R&D services revenue behaves.

source_hints.source: National Nanotechnology Initiative (NNI) annual budget supplement
source_hints.why: Federal nanotech funding levels — a large share of small-firm revenue here is federally funded; sizing the funding base beats guessing firm revenue.
Small nanotech R&D firms are heavily SBIR-dependent; award data evidences revenue concentration and the grant-funded business model.

source_hints.source: Census Business Trends and Outlook Survey (BTOS) AI supplement
source_hints.why: Official AI-use rates by sector as an adoption anchor; no nanotech-specific AI adoption survey is likely to exist.

source_hints.source: KippsDeSanto or similar aerospace/defense/government-services M&A coverage
source_hints.why: Federally funded R&D shops trade in the government-services M&A market; relevant if the dominant sub-segment is defense/agency-funded contract R&D.

capture_questions: Contract R&D here is priced as cost-plus/cost-reimbursable government awards, fixed-price SBIR grants, or commercial NRE/FTE-rate agreements — under cost-plus and FTE-rate structures, AI-driven hour reductions flow to the sponsor, not the firm. Who keeps AI savings under each mechanism, and what is the revenue split across them for EBITDA-positive firms in this code?
Who owns the customer relationship — the firm's contract vehicles and facility approvals, or individual principal investigators whose grants and reputations are personally portable?
What stops sponsors (agencies, corporate R&D departments) from using AI plus their own labs to insource the analytical/design portion, leaving only physical characterization work outsourced?
Is there recurring commercial revenue (materials supply, testing services, licensing) or is revenue one-shot grants and projects — and does AI shorten funded project scopes over time?
terminal_value_question: The mechanism that holds paid demand here is physical: synthesis, fabrication and characterization require cleanrooms and instruments AI cannot replace, while AI-driven simulation and literature work shifts value away from the analytical hours firms bill. Does the dominant sub-segment's revenue sit in the physical-lab slice (durable) or the analysis/design slice (replaceable) — and does federally funded demand, which sets most budgets here, get recalibrated downward as agencies assume AI-accelerated research productivity?
special_notes: HETEROGENEOUS R&D CODE: 541713 spans nanomaterials developers, semiconductor-adjacent research shops, instrument-linked labs and grant-funded ventures. Identify the top-3 revenue sub-segments, score the DOMINANT one, and set heterogeneous: true. Best hypothesis, flagged as hypothesis: materials/semiconductor-adjacent contract R&D and nanomaterials development is the dominant EBITDA-generating cluster.

DATA ODDITY — treat labor_share with suspicion: the record shows labor_share 0.0676 (QCEW 2024 wages $3.6B vs SUSB 2022 receipts $56.9B), implausibly low for an R&D services code and inconsistent with siblings (541714: 0.90, 541715: 0.49). Likely a classification mismatch — a few very large enterprises' receipts booked at this code in SUSB (note 232 firms in the 500+ class out of only 1,469 total) without matching QCEW wage records. Do not re-research the input (rule 6), but do not build V on it at face value; flag in top_uncertain_inputs.

Business-model trap: many firms in this code are venture/grant-funded research ventures with no meaningful EBITDA — the $1-10M EBITDA band framing only fits the fee-for-service subset. The n_band of 301 (quality ESTIMATE, derived from a generic 15.65% services margin) likely overstates genuinely acquirable cash-flowing firms; probe what an EBITDA-positive nanotech 'business' actually is before scoring B.
Pricing-model deflation candidate: federal cost-reimbursable awards and FTE-based commercial research agreements pass AI hour-savings to the sponsor — model in pi_dist with the mechanism stated.

Boundary confusion: 541714 (biotech, incl. nanobiotech boundary disputes), 541715 (general physical/engineering R&D), 334xxx (semiconductor manufacturing, where firms with pilot production get reclassified) — market reports on 'nanotechnology' span products and services across all of these; check definitions before quoting.
